The user wants a low-friction loop for adding real-world AI-CLI output as fixtures that are simultaneously (a) inspectable inputs for refining the cleanup rules and (b) regression tests pinning the desired output. Examples will accumulate over time.

The infrastructure already exists. TestCleanFromTestdata at internal/clean/clean_test.go:167 auto-discovers any testdata/*_sample.txt file and pairs it with a sibling *_expected.txt. Adding a pair already requires zero code changes — but a few small adjustments will make the workflow scale and remove paper cuts:

1. Dedicated folder. Move pairs into testdata/examples/ so this fixture kind is distinct from anything added later.
2. Source-agnostic naming. The heuristics don't care what tool produced the output, so filenames should describe the characteristic being tested, not the source. (Confirmed with the user: rename existing claude_* / copilot_* pairs.)
3. -update golden-file flag. Standard Go pattern: run the test with -update to (re)generate _expected.txt from current Clean() output. Without -update, missing-expected becomes a hard failure with a helpful hint, instead of a silent t.Skipf.
4. Workflow docs. Add a short "Adding example test cases" section to CLAUDE.md so future sessions (and humans) know the loop.

The rule files in internal/clean/*.go are not modified by this change — they're refined as a result of this workflow when a sample reveals a gap.
